scenario.name: research_ops_platform
scenario.theme: Launching an applied research platform that must ship product value while preserving experimental rigor.
scenario.release_need: A research and product organization needs one operating plan that can move experiments into production without losing reproducibility, auditability, or decision quality.
scenario.audience_takeaway: The proposal connects experiment design, evidence review, and product rollout into one repeatable system.
scenario.headline: Applied Research Delivery Operating Plan
strategy_plan: Align experiment throughput with release evidence rather than running research and product on separate tracks.
Use one scorecard for value, reproducibility, and safety before promotion to production.
Package experiment results into operating decisions that non-research stakeholders can use.
business_summary: Move from isolated experiments to a governed release pipeline.
Treat reproducibility and benchmark history as launch requirements.
Use staged release criteria so research wins can survive real production scrutiny.
critical_risks: Research signals look promising but do not translate to production operating constraints.
Benchmarks are strong but decision-makers cannot inspect the supporting evidence quickly enough.
Experimental branches proliferate faster than governance can review them.

pillars.title: Research Throughput
pillars.summary: Increase experiment velocity without breaking comparability.
pillars.integration: Route candidate ideas into benchmark-ready release packets.

pillars.title: Governed Promotion
pillars.summary: Promote only the candidates that pass quality and safety gates.
pillars.integration: Tie release promotion to the same benchmark trail.

pillars.title: Decision Readout
pillars.summary: Convert experiment evidence into board-readable launch claims.
pillars.integration: Attach lab and runtime evidence to the operating memo.

actions: Stabilize scenario suites and quality gates.
Define success metrics before scaling experiments.
Ship the winning candidate with monitoring hooks.
Track post-launch drift against lab expectations.

identity.name: Agent Harness Studio
identity.one_liner: Agent Harness Studio turns one user request into an auditable, benchmarked, and ecosystem-portable agent product.
identity.differentiator: Single pipeline with runtime routing evidence, research-grade release gating, and OpenAI/Anthropic skill export compatibility.

records.title: Experiment Promotion Criteria
records.summary: Applied research candidates should only be promoted when reproducibility, benchmark stability, and operating constraints are jointly satisfied.
records.content: Promotion requires repeatable gains, stable confidence intervals, and a production-facing readout that names risk and rollback assumptions.

records.title: Evidence Packet Template
records.summary: A release packet should combine quantitative metrics, control decisions, citations, unresolved risks, and expansion triggers in one artifact.
records.content: Decision-makers should not have to collect metrics, controls, and supporting citations from separate systems before approving launch.

records.title: Model Context Protocol Architecture
records.summary: Reference for interoperable agent-tool integration and external capability composition.

records.title: LangGraph Overview
records.summary: Operational patterns for stateful agent execution, durability, and orchestration loops.

records.title: Audit Readiness Checklist for AI Service Launches
records.summary: Launch packets need retained traces, policy versioning, evidence retention, and reviewer attribution before expansion.
records.content: Capture request trace, model version, control decision, human approver, and exception handling path in the release packet.

records.title: NIST AI Risk Management Framework
records.summary: Baseline framing for govern, map, measure, and manage controls in AI deployments.

harness.delivery_brief_excerpt:
Applied Research Delivery Operating Plan
Scenario: Launching an applied research platform that must ship product value while preserving experimental rigor.
Decision: go (all_quality_gates_passed)
Request: Design an applied research promotion pack for a model-evaluation platform, balancing experiment throughput, reproducibility, benchmark credibility, and production release criteria.
Operating Thesis:
- The proposal connects experiment design, evidence review, and product rollout into one repeatable system.
Business Summary:
- Move from isolated experiments to a governed release pipeline.
- Treat reproducibility and benchmark history as launch requirements.
- Use staged release criteria so research wins can survive real production scrutiny.
- Evidence base: 12 records and 6 citations were injected into the launch packet.
Phased Rollout:
- Phase 1 - Benchmark Foundation: Stabilize scenario suites and quality gates., Define success metrics before scaling experiments., understand intent
- Phase 2 - Candidate Promotion: Run contenders through repeatable evaluation., Package evidence for decision review., collect supporting signals
- Phase 3 - Production Rollout: Ship the winning candidate with monitoring hooks., Track post-launch drift against lab expectations., synthesize and evaluate
Expected Impact:
- Experiment throughput: value index 82.5 and band gold
- Evidence quality: release decision go with reason all_quality_gates_passed
- Release confidence: completion score 1.00 and tool success 1.00
- Stakeholder trust: stakeholder packet ready for product, operations, and governance review
Critical Risks:
- Research signals look promising but do not translate to production operating constraints.
- Benchmarks are strong but decision-makers cannot inspect the supporting evidence quickly enough.
- Experimental branches proliferate faster than governance can review them.
Evidence Citations:
- internal://research/experiment-promotion-criteria
- internal://cross/evidence-packet-template
- https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-06-18/architecture/index
- https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langgraph/overview
Execution Backbone:
- understand intent
- collect supporting signals
- synthesize and evaluate
- recipe:research-rig

mission.name: research_pack
mission.title: Research Mission Pack
mission.summary: Research-facing package for study design, evidence review, and promotion decisions.
mission.primary_deliverable: Research promotion packet with experiment rationale, evidence, and release criteria.
mission.query: Design an applied research promotion pack for a model-evaluation platform, balancing experiment throughput, reproducibility, benchmark credibility, and production release criteria.
target_users: The proposal connects experiment design, evidence review, and product rollout into one repeatable system.

deliverables.title: Research Brief
deliverables.description: Hypothesis, operating thesis, and study implications.

deliverables.title: Benchmark Readout
deliverables.description: Release gate, leaderboard, and evidence trail.

deliverables.title: Promotion Checklist
deliverables.description: What must pass before the result becomes default.

deliverables.title: Evidence Packet
deliverables.description: External and internal citations linked to the claim.

review_questions: Is the claimed gain reproducible beyond the current scenario set?
Which missing evidence would most likely change the promotion decision?
What post-launch monitoring is required to validate the lab result?
Does the evidence packet support expansion beyond research_ops_platform?
Is AnalysisAgent still the right owner if constraints tighten?

benchmark_targets.name: GAIA
benchmark_targets.strength: Reasoning + retrieval alignment is directly relevant.

benchmark_targets.name: TheAgentCompany
benchmark_targets.strength: Useful for broader knowledge-work research tasks.

benchmark_targets.name: WebArena
benchmark_targets.strength: Only partial overlap through retrieval and action planning.

mission.honest_boundary: Current strength is evidence-backed planning, governance framing, and packaged delivery. It is not yet a leaderboard winner on web navigation or code-fix benchmarks because the repo still lacks full browser-actuation loops and code-task specific execution traces.

archetypes.name: deep-research
archetypes.description: Strong research loop and analysis depth; weaker productization and onboarding.

archetypes.name: data-flow
archetypes.description: Strong flow orchestration and deterministic pipelines; weaker portability.

archetypes.name: skill-hub
archetypes.description: Strong ecosystem integration; weaker rigorous evaluation gates.
